The pricing seems reasonable for professional software, but I need to see the technical specifications and system requirements first. No point paying for something that will bog down my workstation.
These testimonials look good, but I want to see what the negative reviews say about reliability issues. Real-world performance matters more than marketing claims.
This interface looks unnecessarily complicated. I don't have time to learn a bloated system when I need something that just works reliably for my technical tasks.
They want my email just to see the full feature list? That's a red flag. If they can't be transparent about basic functionality, how can I trust the technical implementation?
Finally, clear documentation with actual technical details. This looks like it was built by people who understand professional workflows, not just marketing requirements.
